steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student’s stated goal is to explain how the House of Wisdom preserved knowledge — the method or process, not just the fact that it existed or who worked there. The correct answer must trace the steps or mechanism of preservation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The House of Wisdom was known for bringing together knowledge from around the world, including from Greece, India, and China.': This mentions sources of knowledge but does not explain the preservation process — what was done with those texts once collected. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Founded in Iraq in the eighth century, the House of Wisdom employed many scholars as translators.': This introduces the scholars but describes their role only vaguely and omits the full chain of steps (collection, translation, paper versions) that constitute preservation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Writings from the Greek philosopher Aristotle and the Indian mathematician Aryabhata were preserved at the House of Wisdom.': This names specific examples of preserved texts but never explains how preservation happened. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The House of Wisdom collected writings from different countries and created paper versions in Arabic to be studied and shared.': This traces the actual process: collect texts $\Rightarrow$ translate into Arabic $\Rightarrow$ create paper copies for study and distribution. That directly explains how preservation was accomplished. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
